title: Statistical-methods + provenance + suppression standard
context: Disparities-Analysis produces reproducible cancer outcome disparity analyses from aggregate, open-access public data. Every quantitative claim must stand up to peer-level scrutiny: correct age-standardization, confidence intervals, small-cell suppression, both absolute and relative disparity measures, and explicit limitations. This standard is built first alongside the framing policy (disparities-analysis-framing-001) as a hard product requirement. No epidemiology/biostatistics reviewer is yet secured.
objective: Write the authoritative statistical-methods, provenance, and suppression standard that all analyses must follow. The standard mandates: age-standardization (2000 US standard population for US data; WHO World Standard for international); confidence intervals (Tiwari-modified for SEER rates); RSE/stability flagging; joinpoint trend modeling with APC/AAPC; both absolute (rate difference) and relative (rate ratio) disparity plus the HD*Calc measure set (Index of Disparity, SII/RII with CIs) validated against HD*Calc on the exemplar site; an 'adjusted-for / not-adjusted-for' one-line note on every disparity statement; exact mechanically-checkable suppression thresholds (numerator < 10, flag RSE > 30%, suppress RSE > 50%, complementary suppression, joinpoint minimum-points rule); per-group direction-of-bias notes for race/ethnicity misclassification; and pre-decided OMB 1997-vs-2024 trend-bridging.
acceptanceCriteria: Mandates age-standardization using the 2000 US standard population for domestic data and the WHO World Standard for international comparisons, with the standard population recorded in every Source record
Requires confidence intervals on all rates (e.g. Tiwari-modified method for SEER rates) and RSE-based stability flags: flag unreliable at RSE > 30%, suppress at RSE > 50%
Mandates joinpoint trend modeling (NCI Joinpoint Regression Program) with APC and AAPC reported for all trend comparisons, including a minimum-points rule for the joinpoint model

Requires both absolute (rate difference) and relative (rate ratio) disparity measures on every disparity statement — never relative-only; adopts the HD*Calc measure set (Index of Disparity, SII, RII with CIs) and validates pipeline outputs against HD*Calc on the exemplar site
Requires a one-line 'adjusted for / not adjusted for' note on every disparity statement (e.g. age-standardized controls age but not stage, comorbidity, or access) to prevent over-interpretation
Defines the Source, DisparityMeasure, and Assertion provenance records and enforces 'no quantitative assertion without a Source' as a mechanically-checkable rule wired into CI

Defines exact, mechanically-checkable small-cell suppression thresholds: suppress numerator < 10 (NCHS-style); apply complementary suppression so suppressed cells cannot be reconstructed from margins; honor SEER/USCS thresholds where more conservative; set a joinpoint minimum-points rule

Records a per-group direction-of-bias note for race/ethnicity misclassification (which groups are under-counted and why, per CDC/USCS notes for AIAN, API, and Hispanic people; IHS linkage for AIAN mentioned)
Pre-decides OMB 1997-vs-2024 race/ethnicity standard handling (combined question, added MENA category) so pre/post trends are bridged with documented methodology rather than silently mis-compared
Signed off by the epidemiology/biostatistics reviewer (recorded in the reviewers ledger)
output: A reviewed methods-specification document defining the project's statistical-methods, provenance-record, and suppression standards — the contract that disparities-analysis-rates-009, disparities-analysis-disparity-010, and all subsequent analyses are reviewed against.
